stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML topics including transformers and text-generation.
The presence of Llama indicates relevance to the rapidly evolving large language model landscape.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the core field of NLP/Generative AI, specifically text generation.
The associated tags indicate its relevance within the Hugging Face ecosystem (transformers, safetensors).

stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The paper introduces EurekAgent, an approach focusing on Agent Environment Engineering for autonomous scientific discovery.
LLM-based agents are capable of proposing, validating, and iterating scientific solutions, sometimes surpassing human-designed methods.
The core argument is that the current bottleneck in autonomous science is shifting from agent workflow design to designing the agent's operational environment (resources, constraints, interfaces).

stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The research introduces 'EurekAgent,' an LLM-based agent designed to automate scientific discovery.
The study posits that the primary bottleneck for autonomous scientific discovery is shifting from agent workflow design to designing the agent environment (resources, constraints, interfaces).
These agents have demonstrated the ability to propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions that can outperform human-designed methods.

stories.title: AUR Packages Compromised with Infostealer and Rootkit
stories.author: keyle
bullet_points: AUR packages have been compromised, indicating a significant security vulnerability in a popular software distribution method.
The compromise involves the deployment of both an infostealer and a rootkit, suggesting sophisticated malware.
This represents a critical security threat to users relying on the AUR ecosystem.

stories.title: German court ruling declares Google's AI Overviews are Google's own words and makes it liable for false answers
stories.author: the-decoder.com via gspr
bullet_points: A German court ruled that Google's AI Overviews are considered Google's own generated content.
The ruling establishes legal liability for Google regarding false or inaccurate answers provided by its AI.
This decision signals growing regulatory scrutiny over the accuracy and authorship of generative AI outputs.

stories.title: iOS 27 On-Device AI and the Hardware-Gated Edge Inference Split
stories.author: cnchenkai
bullet_points: The article discusses a new architectural division within Apple Intelligence, specifically concerning on-device AI capabilities.
A key theme is the 'Hardware-Gated Edge Inference Split,' indicating a strategic balance between local and cloud processing.
The news is highly relevant as it details a significant, forward-looking shift in how major platforms are implementing on-device AI.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, now integrates MLX for enhanced performance.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads for Apple hardware.
This feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a new personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core feature emphasizes running all AI processes entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: BMW’s Neue Klasse M concept brings next-gen EV tech to motorsports
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: BMW is debuting its Neue Klasse M concept, showcasing next-generation EV technology.
The concept introduces a new design language for BMW's M performance division.
The launch signals a strategic shift by BMW toward a more electrified future in motorsports.

stories.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Agents-K1 introduces a new pipeline for scientific knowledge orchestration, addressing limitations in current LLM research agents.
Existing agents often simplify scientific papers too much (e.g., using only abstracts or citation counts), losing crucial details.
The new system converts raw documents into 'agent-native' scientific knowledge, capturing entities, claims, evidence, and method lineages.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Harnesses
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) demonstrate that recursion over model calls is effective for long-context reasoning.
The research introduces the 'Recursive Agent Harness,' defining a pattern where the recursive unit is a full agent (with tools/filesystem) rather than just a model call.
This work connects academic RLM research with practical applications seen in production coding agents, such as those in Anthropic's dynamic workflows.

stories.title: I Lead AI Agents Every Day - Here Are 5 Shifts No Standard Tells You How to Make
stories.author: itskondrat
bullet_points: Google DeepMind is investing $10M into multi-agent safety research.
The article discusses non-standard approaches to leading AI agents.
The topic highlights the growing industry focus on AI safety in complex agent systems.

stories.title: We Audited the Viral 213k-Star "Everything Claude Code" Repo — and Found a Malware Clone in the Wild
stories.author: joergmichno
bullet_points: A highly popular repository, 'Everything Claude Code,' was audited.
The audit revealed the presence of a malware clone within the widely starred project.
This highlights significant security risks associated with viral, unvetted open-source AI code.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core ML/NLP tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating its generative capabilities.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher quality model outputs, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware setups.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving capabilities on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run entirely on the user's local hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Cameras, sensors, and 3D body scans: All the tech helping eliminate blown calls
stories.author: Ben Dowsett, WIRED.com
bullet_points: Referees will utilize digital twins of players during the World Cup.
Advanced camera, sensor, and 3D body scanning technology is being deployed.
The technology aims to improve officiating by allowing comprehensive, multi-angle play review.

stories.title: Pokémon Go players unwittingly contributed to tech with military drone uses
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Pokémon Go data is being repurposed for AI training, raising ethical concerns.
The data usage has been linked to potential military drone applications.
The story highlights the scrutiny surrounding the use of consumer data in advanced AI systems.

stories.title: Verizon sent man a refurbished phone with MDM, then deleted his data remotely
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Verizon shipped a refurbished phone to a customer that was equipped with Mobile Device Management (MDM).
The carrier subsequently deleted the customer's data remotely from the device.
The incident highlights potential security and preparation flaws in how Verizon handles refurbished devices.

stories.title: After nearly breaking, NASA's Deep Space Network "worked well" on Artemis II
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: NASA's Deep Space Network (DSN) performed effectively during the Artemis II mission.
The report suggests that operational usage of the DSN exceeded documented specifications.
The finding indicates potential operational flexibility or undocumented capabilities within the network.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for complex reasoning.
It proposes a new framework called Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT).
RA-RFT aims to improve reasoning by moving beyond simple lexical or semantic similarity in knowledge retrieval.

stories.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
stories.author: C. Karen Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the complex challenge of dexterous manipulation using articulated tools in robotics.
Introduces Mana, a general sim-to-real framework designed to tackle this complexity.
Focuses on overcoming the difficulty of learning functional grasping policies for physically complex tools.

stories.title: SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
stories.author: Min-Hung Chen
bullet_points: The research addresses the fundamental challenge of spatial reasoning in Vision-Language Models (VLMs).
It investigates how the design of the 'action interface' limits the effectiveness of tool-augmented agents.
The work specifically studies how this interface design shapes an agent's capacity for open-ended 3D spatial reasoning.

stories.title: Automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models
stories.author: Stefan Feuerriegel
bullet_points: The research proposes using Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate the assessment of research reproducibility.
This addresses the resource-intensive and difficult scaling issues associated with traditional, manual reproducibility checks in social and behavioral sciences.
The study validates the LLM approach by comparing its generated analyses against original findings across 76 published studies.

stories.title: Before You Think: System 0, AI-Mediated Cognition and Cognitive Colonization
stories.author: Giuseppe Riva
bullet_points: The paper analyzes three frameworks (Tri-System Theory, Thinkframes, and System 0) for understanding AI's cognitive impact.
It posits that 'System 0' offers a unique theoretical perspective on AI's influence that other models cannot fully capture.
The core concept introduced is 'cognitive colonization,' suggesting AI systems fundamentally alter human cognition.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, providing deep evaluation for this low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to embedding tasks.

stories.title: Beyond Runtime Enforcement: Shield Synthesis as Defensibility Analysis for Adversarial Networks
stories.author: Sultan Almuhammadi
bullet_points: The paper re-frames 'shielded reinforcement learning' from a runtime safety mechanism to a design-time analytical tool.
It argues that the underlying automata-theoretic machinery provides structural insights into adversarial networks rather than just action constraints.
The methodology involves specification compilation, product game construction, and winning-region extraction for analysis.

stories.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
stories.author: C. Karen Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the complex challenge of dexterous robotics involving articulated tool manipulation.
Introduces 'Mana' (Manipulation Animator), a general sim-to-real framework for this task.
Focuses on overcoming the difficulty of learning functional grasping policies for physically complex, articulated tools.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for identifying compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
This method tests if an LLM's direct answer matches the answer derived from composing a provided decomposition of the same query.
It offers a complementary approach to existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency) by leveraging formal concepts from operad theory.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, a comprehensive MTEB-style benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, significantly increasing coverage for this low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to embedding tasks.

stories.title: The Stable Recovery Manifold: Geometric Principles Governing Recoverability in Continual Learning
stories.author: Bhavika Melwani
bullet_points: The research investigates the geometric principles behind knowledge recoverability in continual learning, moving beyond the concept of catastrophic forgetting.
A new metric, Recovery Subspace Dimensionality (k_t), is introduced to quantify the complexity of recovering previously learned knowledge.
The study validates its findings using a sequentially trained ResNet-18 on Split CIFAR-100, analyzing representational drift and recovery across ten tasks.

stories.title: Aerial Wildfire Suppression Planning with a Hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata Fire Model
stories.author: Anthony Wong
bullet_points: Introduces a novel framework for aerial wildfire suppression planning.
Combines a hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata model to predict spatially varying fire spread.
Uses gradient-based design to optimize targeted aerial intervention strategies.

stories.title: Beyond Runtime Enforcement: Shield Synthesis as Defensibility Analysis for Adversarial Networks
stories.author: Sultan Almuhammadi
bullet_points: The paper recontextualizes 'shielded reinforcement learning' from a runtime safety mechanism to a design-time analytical tool.
It argues that the underlying automata-theoretic machinery provides structural insights into adversarial networks rather than just imposing action constraints.
The methodology involves specification compilation, product game construction, and attractor computation to analyze system defensibility.

stories.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
stories.author: Zhiyuan Hu
bullet_points: Introduces EvoArena, a new benchmark suite designed to test LLM agents in dynamic environments.
Addresses a key limitation of current LLM evaluations, which often assume static operational environments.
Focuses on the necessity for agents to continuously adapt knowledge and behavior to progressive environmental changes.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for complex reasoning tasks.
It argues that simple lexical or semantic similarity retrieval is insufficient for capturing underlying reasoning patterns.
A new framework, Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT), is proposed as a post-training solution.

stories.title: Influcoder: Distilling Decoders' Gradient Influence Rankings into an Encoder for Data Attribution
stories.author: Pascal Denis
bullet_points: The research introduces 'Influcoder,' a method to create an encoder for Data Attribution (DA).
DA methods aim to quantify how specific training data samples influence a model's output, such as identifying sources of toxic behavior in LLMs.
The technique distills gradient influence rankings from decoders into the new encoder structure.

stories.title: HyperTool: Beyond Step-Wise Tool Calls for Tool-Augmented Agents
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the 'execution-granularity mismatch' in tool-augmented LLM agents.
Proposes 'HyperTool,' a unified, executable MCP-style tool interface.
Aims to reduce context consumption by abstracting low-level, step-wise tool calls from the main reasoning trace.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for identifying compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
This method leverages operad theory—a formalism for iterated substitution—to check if an LLM's direct answer matches the answer derived from a stated decomposition of the same query.
It offers a complementary approach to existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency) by diagnosing internal reasoning structure rather than just output uncertainty.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, significantly increasing coverage depth for Slovak.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models on embedding tasks.

stories.title: Operads for compositional reasoning in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'operads' as a mathematical framework to formally model question decomposition in Large Language Models (LLMs).
Question decomposition, breaking complex queries into simpler sub-queries, is a known technique for improving LLM reasoning but currently lacks a rigorous mathematical basis.
The proposed 'questions operad' ($Q$) provides a structure to describe how multiple inputs can be combined into a single, composed output.

stories.title: Why New Language Features Need to Target AI Agents, Not Developers
stories.author: moderncpp
bullet_points: The article argues that language design should shift focus from human developers to AI agents.
The premise is that AI agents are increasingly capable of writing production code.
It calls for optimizing language features specifically for bots rather than just providing syntax sugar for humans.
